Empires & Puzzles Wolfgang Review: 5-Star Legendary Holy Yellow from Clash of Knights Family

Wolfgang isn't your average healer. He hits first, reduces damage, and then... you wait. Is his massive delayed health boost worth the anxiety in today's fast-paced meta? We break down the Commander of the Castle and his unique high-risk, high-reward playstyle.
Wolfgang: The Ticking Time Bomb of Health

Wolfgang is a 5-Star Legendary Holy hero from the Clash of Knights Alliance Quest, originally released back in 2021. In an era where instant gratification is king, Wolfgang asks you to have a little patience. He is a hybrid damage dealer and healer who operates on a delay timer, making him one of the more unique—and stressful—heroes to use. While he was once a terrifying sight on defense, power creep has not been kind to his base stats. This review focuses on his
; his costume, which changes his class and healing mechanic, is reviewed separately.

Wolfgang looks exactly like what his title suggests: a "Commander of the Castle." He is clad in heavy, ornate blue and gold armor, holding a helmet that looks like it's seen better days. His rugged, bearded face and glowing eye give him the vibe of a veteran who has survived countless sieges. Compared to his costume, which looks more ceremonial and regal, this version feels more battle-hardened. He definitely didn't skip shoulder day with those massive pauldrons.

To put it bluntly, Wolfgang's base stats are ancient history. With a power of 816 and defensive stats hovering around 780, he is significantly behind modern 5-star heroes who now boast base stats well over 1300. Even with a full limit break, his numbers barely scratch the surface of what a modern hero brings to the table out of the box. He relies heavily on his damage reduction and Fighter class to survive long enough to do his job.
Wolfgang's skill is a three-part act:
He deals 250% damage to all enemies. In today's meta, this is a "soften them up" hit rather than a finisher, but chip damage adds up.
received damage by -50% for 4 turns. This is crucial because...
After 4 turns, he boosts the health of all allies by 600.

The mechanic is simple: if Wolfgang survives for 4 turns, your team gets a massive overheal. If he dies or gets dispelled, you get nothing. The damage reduction helps him survive, but it leaves the rest of your team vulnerable. The Fighter class is his saving grace here—even if he dies, a timely revive can keep the timer going (or at least keep him in the fight to cast again).
Average (10 tiles). With a level 23 Mana Troop (or equivalent bonuses), this drops to 9 tiles.
250% to all enemies. Against a modern team with ~1500 defense, this won't be wiping the board, but it disrupts formations.
600 Boosted Health. This is a flat value, meaning it's unaffected by healing reduction ailments that target % based healing, but it's a "all or nothing" burst.
-50% damage reduction effectively doubles his HP against direct damage, but it does nothing against status ailments like DoT.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Wolf Family bonus is one of the best in the game for defensive teams. A 35%/50% chance to make a cast buff undispellable is huge. If this triggers on his damage reduction, he becomes significantly harder to kill, guaranteeing the heal goes off unless he's brute-forced down.
Wolfgang does not have any innate passives in his base form.

At the start of battle, his attack is increased by 20% for 6 turns. This helps his initial hit sting a bit more, but frankly, a defensive Aether Power like Damage Reduction or Defense Up would have suited his kit much better.
Upon release, Wolfgang was a menace. The combination of damage and a massive overheal made him a staple in war defenses, often paired with Ludwig or other Wolf family members.

The game has moved on. Heroes now heal for more instantly, or deal significantly more damage. Waiting 4 turns for a 600 heal feels like an eternity when battles can end in 3 turns. His stats make him squishy by 2026 standards.
He can still find use in Very Fast wars where he can fire quickly, or in PvE events where you can control the board. In competitive PvP, he is a liability unless heavily protected.
, but the costume offers a significant pivot.
Base is Fighter (Revive), Costume is Paladin (Defense Up). The Fighter class is arguably better for the base version's "survive to heal" mechanic.

The Costume switches from a delayed burst heal to a Heal Over Time (HoT) that starts immediately. This makes the Costume much safer and more reliable, though it loses the big "turn-around" potential of the burst heal.
The costume provides a massive stat boost that the base version desperately needs.

If you have the costume, the stat bonus alone usually makes it the superior choice, even if you prefer the Fighter class. However, the Costume's immediate HoT is generally preferred in the current fast-paced meta over the risky delayed heal.
Maximizes his chances of surviving the crucial 4 turns to trigger the heal. Increases the effectiveness of his damage reduction.
His AoE hit will be weaker (approx 200-250 damage per target).
He becomes very fragile, risking the loss of his heal entirely.
path is recommended. Wolfgang's primary value is his heal; his damage is secondary. If he dies, he provides zero healing.
600 boosted health is a significant chunk, capable of swinging a match if it lands.
The chance to make buffs undispellable is a game-changer and protects his damage reduction.
The chance to revive can clutch a match, keeping the heal timer alive or allowing him to fire again.
The 4-turn wait is agonizingly long. He can be killed or dispelled before the heal ever triggers.
He only protects himself with damage reduction, leaving teammates exposed.
His base stats are too low to survive sustained fire from modern snipers without significant emblem/LB investment.
Unless the family bonus triggers, a simple dispel ruins his entire kit.
His Average speed becomes Very Fast, allowing him to set up his heal early and often.
The delayed heal is easy to manage in PvE, effectively keeping your team topped up between waves.
He provides a buff to himself, and his heal (if it lands) counts as a positive effect.
Wolfgang + Ludwig + Quintin. The family bonus makes their buffs undispellable 50% of the time, making Ludwig and Wolfgang a nightmare to remove.
Wolfgang + Prof. Lidenbrock + Sif. A yellow stack focused on overheating and damage reduction/counterattack.
Sabina (especially Costume), Melendor, and modern dispellers can wipe his damage reduction and cancel the pending heal.
Mist or Franz can prevent him from gaining his damage reduction, making him an easy target.
While his heal is boosted (and thus ignores some blocks), preventing the buff application stops the heal from queuing up.

If you pull Wolfgang early, he is a fantastic "carry" hero. His overheal will effectively double your team's HP in map stages. Don't worry about his outdated stats for PvE; he is still a monster there.

He is likely a depth hero for War teams or specific Tournaments. Do not prioritize Alpha Aethers on him unless you are a die-hard Clash of Knights fan or lack modern healers. He simply doesn't survive well enough in the top 1000 to justify the resources.
"Wolfgang is the definition of high anxiety. I spend 4 turns sweating bullets hoping he doesn't get dispelled."
"The Fighter revive saved me more times than I can count. He dies, revives, and boom—health for everyone."
"He used to be a tank, now he's barely a flank. Time comes for us all."
Wolfgang is a relic of a different time, where battles were slower and strategy revolved around timing big effects. While his

suffers from outdated stats and a risky mechanic, he remains a fun, thematic hero for those who enjoy the "Wolf Pack" synergy. If you have him, dress him in his Costume if possible—but don't underestimate the clutch potential of a reviving Fighter who just won't stay dead.
A retired general. Respect his service, but maybe don't put him on the front lines of your defense team.
A comprehensive review of Wolfgang, the legendary Holy hero from the Clash of Knights event. We analyze his stats, special skill, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
